  • Allie1 day ago

    I beg....please....a transparent option. You're making the better traveler of my dreams and you'd have it in a ghost style? Please. And maybe a larger screen add on. I would pay extra.

    • Caleb1 day ago

      I’d agree to both of those options.

    • KateCollaborator1 day ago

      Hello Allie,

      Thank you for your support and for this suggestion! A clear transparent option would look really cool :)

      I think Adam has some more information about why a larger screen would cause us to need to increase costs pretty significantly or there would be an impact on battery life or lag...

      Please let me know if you have any other questions, I'm more than happy to help :)

      Sincerely,

      Kate

      kate@zerowriter.ink

    • ZerowriterCreator1 day ago

      Hello! Adam here. Yeah: for this project going smoothly it’s important to keep the screen and major components streamlined, so I won’t be adding any larger screen options. You can check the FAQ for a more detailed write-up on why we are using this display.

      But, transparent/clear — hmm..

  • Jody Ayres1 day ago

    Have you thought about a padded carrying case for it? That would be good to protect it.

    Showing 3 of 4 replies
    • Lucas Keefer1 day ago

      Would add a case or sleeve to my pledge in a heartbeat

    • KateCollaborator1 day ago

      Hello Jody,

      Thank you for the suggestion and your support! We are hoping to potentially add some case options to the Pledge Manager over time... A padded case would be great for those using their Fold on the road...

      Warm regards,

      Kate

    It's exciting seeing a project like this based in Ottawa. I wonder if local pickup could be an option?

    • ZerowriterCreator2 days ago

      100% — we’re in the downtown core, pickup is absolutely doable

      Definitely! We'll meet at Rideau (Ottawa ball knowledge required for that one).

      Nice, thanks!

  • Steff Leigh3 days ago

    Super excited about this, and I also like the fun surprise indie book add-on. I've shared a link to that program sign up on Mastodon/Fediverse, as there are a lot of indie authors on there who might be interested. :)

    • KateCollaborator3 days ago

      Thank you for spreading the word, Steff! We think it is pretty awesome, too :)

  • Noah Chinn3 days ago

    The one thing I feel I absolutely need for something like this is a way to sync to my Dropbox, which is where I keep my work. You mentioned possible future support for Google Drive... but wouldn't you have to add a wi-fi connection to do that? Or is it a matter of plugging it into a computer that is connected to wi-fi and it syncing up that way? What are the plans there?

    • ZerowriterCreator3 days ago

      Hi Noah! Great question. I'll explain. The Zerowriter Fold has the guts that allow for wifi. They are disabled by default. If the user wants, they can enable it in the settings. Wifi will only be used for sending documents to a connected service. The only service I plan on building support for is google drive, but any could be added. Supporting cloud services is tedious and difficult because they change often, so my hope is by providing the structure via the most popular one, other developers can contribute and we can support other services.

      Hope this makes sense. Basically: I wouldn't want anyone purchasing this to expect a highly connected ecosystem of syncing. That is what the others in this space are good at, and at a higher price. But, we can start simple.

    • Noah Chinn3 days ago

      Thanks for that update. While I do have a Google Drive account, I've been using Dropbox since it came out for my work. Still, shouldn't be too hard for me to move stuff over if that's all that ends up supported.

  • Kitty Ho3 days ago

    Please add other languages! I wish it supported Chinese input!

    • ZerowriterCreator3 days ago

      Hi Kitty! Chinese Input is on the roadmap. We intend to make it, but we can't promise it will be ready when we ship, as it requires some proper development time.

  • moggyhoho3 days ago

    I love this can support screenplay formatting. I know it has push to gdrive, any chance it could support mounting to an smb share, or pushing to something selfhosted? I run a nas that's my central storage for writing projects. It'd be great if that was an option. I can't wait to get mine.

    • ZerowriterCreator3 days ago

      The code will be shared publicly, and other developers can build features, so there's opportunity for other sharing methods, for sure! Self-hosted in smart. SMB is nice and simple, too, with plenty of libraries, so should be doable. Google drive push is in development, I won't promise it, but it will likely be ready to roll in time.

  • Janine Shull3 days ago

    Yay about time another company puts something on the market to really challenge zerowrite traveler which is overly inflated in price. When I first saw the ad for this on FB I was instantly going to fund it. Can't wait to get mine. Thank you for the hard work and can't wait this will be a game changer for this Indie author 😁

    • Janine Shull3 days ago

      I mean freewrite traveler brain fart today. (I'd edit but it won't let me. )

    • KateCollaborator3 days ago

      Thank you so much for your support and for your message, Janine! We can't wait for you to get your Fold, too :)

  • Luis CaparrozSuperbacker3 days ago

    Hi! Excited to back this project! Going with a mechanical keyboard is great, and the options for different switches are pretty welcome (I use Ambient Silent switches on my main keyboard).

    I have a few questions:

    - How is motion/navigation while editing? I mean, does it respond to commands like Ctrl + arrow keys, Shift + arrow keys, Home, End, etc?

    - Overall, it seems very promising, and I'd like to request one feature, which I assume isn't very common. I'm a software developer, and I'm really fond of vim keybindings/mode. Have you considered something like that?

    • ZerowriterCreator3 days ago

      Hello Luis, thank you! Glad you are familiar with Darryl’s switches!

      Navigation: yes. There’s built-in shortcuts for moving between words, lines, and paragraphs. There is shortcuts for jumping to the start or end of documents. Shift works as you would expect for highlighting and editing, if you are in Word Processing mode. I’ll add a more detailed video in the coming weeks going over the full software.

      For VIM controls: I know a lot of people would enjoy this. It isn’t really high on my to-do list, but since the project software will be open source, I hope another developer would build it out or add similar support and share it. It should be totally doable and approachable.

      Thanks a lot!

  • Juan Antonio Mateos4 days ago

    Teclas en español...

    • KateCollaborator3 days ago

      Hello Juan,

      Yes, the Fold supports Spanish, and we'll roll out keycaps for different languages (starting with common Latin languages like German, French, Italian, Scandinavian languages, etc...) after the campaign closes. Via Kickstarter - you will have the option to add to your pledge after the campaign closes as we will be adding keycaps for different languages. If the Spanish keycaps are not available at the time of shipping, your device will come with the stock QWERTY ENG/US keycaps and we can get you the Spanish language keycaps as they become available.

      Also - any Kailh Choc Keycaps are compatible so you could source your own. Our Keycap partner (FKCaps) also produces print-on-demand keycaps that work with ZeroWriter, so we can facilitate a Spanish Keyboard for you.

      Another route we are exploring is a standard keycap pack for major European languages that includes all necessary characters (umlauts, ß, accented vowels, ç, ø, œ, etc...).

      In short - it won't be a problem re: Spanish-language keycaps. Just a matter of when.

      Please let me know if you have any questions or are curious about anything. I'm happy to help :)

      Sincerely,

      Kate

  • ZerowriterCreator4 days ago

    Hi everyone! Posted an update, and wanted to share that we have stretch goals live. The next one is a pretty big one :)

  • Lauren Ford4 days ago

    Ah, boo. I backed it before the new pictures for the retro color got posted (it was so small I couldn't figure it out), and there was no way to change the pledge afterwards. I assumed it would be a pledge manager thing and not a limited edition at the time thing, as it wasn't made clear until the list was updated after launch and by that time I couldn't change anything.

    If any other colors are considered, I'd love a teal or a pink color. Or more of the purple released.

    • KateCollaborator4 days ago

      Good Morning Lauren,

      I'm very sorry to hear that you were not able to get a Retro Edition Fold... Would you mind reaching out to me via email when you have a second so I can put you on our list of users who would like to order any extra Retro Editions? My address is kate@zerowriter.ink.

      Also, teal and pink would be beautiful colors. I'm thinking a hot pink... But something a bit more muted could be pretty as well. We are hoping to offer some other color options in the future, so suggestions are very welcome :)

      Looking forward to speaking more,

      Kate

      kate@zerowriter.ink

  • Michael Way4 days ago

    Will there be any opportunities for alternative keyboard layouts? I have a strong preference for ISO.

    • Michael Way4 days ago

  • Rafi5 days ago

    Hello, is there a chance of Google drive syncing?

    • ZerowriterCreator5 days ago

      Hi! Sync: no, push: yes! We’ve got Google Drive push (Zerowriter fold -> drive) sorted and it should be available at launch as an option. 2-way Syncing is a bit more complicated and has more room for problems, but we’ll investigate options!

    Super excited about the price and that it's open source.   • Caleb5 days ago

    Super excited to see this Kickstarter take off. I've been wanting a Freewrite Traveler since I first saw it over a year ago, and I am happy to get all the features I wanted and more in a cheaper package.       Warm regards,

      Kate

      kate@zerowriter.ink

    Thank you for developing and offering this clamshell open-source writer's drafting device. The software developer and aspiring author in me cannot thank your team enough!

  • bef5 days ago

    Will it be possible to get it shipped with a German keyboard layout? QWERTZ with Umlauts ÖÄÜ and ß?

    Also what is the computing core? An ESP32 like the Zerowriter Ink? I think the KS page doesn’t state it explicitly or I missed it.

    • ZerowriterCreator5 days ago

      Hey :) Yeah, we'll be adding German keyboard as an option in the Pledge Manager at the end of the campaign. Definitely a popular request!

      The chip is ESP32-S3, so a different chip than Ink but still in the esp32 family. I decided to leave those technical details out of the Kickstarter campaign as it might confuse people expecting laptop-style specs.

    • bef5 days ago

      Great, thanks!
